claim: Command Center moved from passive dashboard callouts to resolver cards that expose next controls, while sales pull metrics were corrected for resale/trending-item decisions.
changes: Added resolver button metadata to cockpit warnings, cautions, and advisories.
Added frontend cockpit action handlers for actions, queue, dormant detection, pattern scans, pull focus, memory search, memory rewire, and surfaced-card cleanup.
Added API endpoint to archive open surfaced cards.
Separated market/resale sales context from CRM pipeline sales context.
Corrected trending-item metrics to use sell-through, acquisition cost, gross margin, days-to-sale, inventory turns, and result logging.
Removed automatic contextual-memory rebuild from startup and kept rewiring user-triggered.

verification.command: scan web/app.js $() references against web/index.html ids

verification.result: warnings, cautions, and advisories returned button arrays
top card was Sales OS latest trending items; metric used sell-through, acquisition cost, gross margin, days-to-sale, inventory turns, and result logging
completed in about 0.21 seconds after removing automatic contextual rewiring

user_value: A user can now see a cockpit warning and press the next operational control immediately, instead of interpreting the warning and manually hunting for the right tab or endpoint.
next_risk: Documentation and proof artifacts must be updated every version so implementation remains legible outside the live chat.
